response.justification: While Bogdan has 15+ years of software development experience and some foundational systems programming skills (C, Linux, low-level work), this is a poor fit for the role. The job explicitly requires native-level Japanese proficiency, which is entirely absent from the resume. Beyond that critical disqualification, his career has progressively shifted toward modern web/backend development (TypeScript, Node.js, React) since 2018, leaving his embedded systems work 6-10 years in the past. Most critically, he shows no evidence of the specialized expertise required: security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal), embedded networking (SNMP, NETCONF, switch operation), or Linux kernel analysis with source-code modification at the firmware level.
strengths: 15+ years of software development experience significantly exceeds minimum 3-year requirement
Historical C/C++ and low-level systems programming background (National Instruments, sound synthesis), plus Linux and DevOps infrastructure knowledge
Strong track record as technical leader with mentoring experience and ability to work across multiple languages and domains
concerns: Complete absence of native-level Japanese language proficiency, which is an explicit critical requirement
Career trajectory moved away from embedded systems toward web/backend development; embedded firmware work ended ~2018
No demonstrated experience with required security technologies (encryption, MACsec, 802.1X, tamper detection) or embedded networking (SNMP, NETCONF, switch operation)
